channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil’s proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 2 teens detained following explosions near Denmark's Israeli Embassy
item.description: Two Swedish teenagers were detained Thursday on preliminary charges in connection with a pair of explosions near the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department takes action against Russian hacking group
item.description: U.S. officials have seized dozens of web domains used by Russian intelligence agents and their proxies to steal Americans’ information, the Justice Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: South Africa's top court will decide if impeachment proceedings against the president can be revived
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South Africa's Constitutional Court said Thursday that it would hear a case next month brought by two opposition parties to revive impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over a scandal involving more than $500,000 in cash that was hidden in a couch a
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum called “deplorable.”.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italian family believes painting found in a dump in 1960s is a Picasso and seeks authentication
item.description: An Italian family hopes to prove definitively that a painting discarded from a villa on Capri more than 60 years ago is a Picasso.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus opposition urges immediate release of over 200 political prisoners in dire state
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Belarusian opposition leader in exile Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya on Thursday said 224 political prisoners in Belarus must be freed urgently, and called on Western countries to negotiate their release with the Belarusian authorities.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Palestinians’ approval of US leadership at record low: Gallup
item.description: The approval rating in Palestine of U.S. leaders has dropped to a record low, a new survey found. Just 5 percent of Palestinians in the West Bank and East Jerusalem approve of U.S. leadership, the lowest on record since 2006, according to a poll conducted by Gallup.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Victor Perahia, Holocaust survivor and president of French Union of Auschwitz Deportees, dies at 91
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Victor Perahia, the president of the French Union of Auschwitz Deportees and a Holocaust survivor, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran warns of 'decisive response' if Israel crosses 'red lines'
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned this week at the Asia Cooperation Dialogue summit that Israel cannot act with "impunity" and "crossing [Iran's] red lines" will be met with a "decisive response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads were flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: The Israeli military has warned people to evacuate a city and other communities in southern Lebanon that are north of a U.N.-declared buffer zone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defies Biden in Lebanon, testing US support
item.description: The Israeli operation in Lebanon is exposing the failures of U.S. efforts to de-escalate the Middle East conflict, raising the likelihood of a full-scale war against Hezbollah and testing President Biden's support for Israel's expanding military campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Rwanda records at least 9 deaths from Marburg outbreak
item.description: Rwanda has declared a public outbreak of the Marburg virus, confirming nearly 30 infections and at least nine deaths as of the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Putin signs law granting immunity to criminal defendants who join the army
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a law Wednesday that will allow criminal defendants to skip prosecution and potential prison time if they enlist in the army.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will Netanyahu 'take the fight directly to Iran' after massive missile strike across Israel?
item.description: Amid the most lethal Iranian missile strike into Israel territory, military experts are urging Israel to take off the gloves and punish the mullahs in Iran with devastating warfare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 dead and thousands evacuated as a typhoon approaches Taiwan
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — An approaching typhoon bringing strong winds and torrential rainfall to Taiwan killed two people and injured dozens of others over the past few days and forced thousands to evacuate from low-lying or mountainous areas.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis' approval of US leadership drops since Oct. 7 Hamas attack
item.description: The approval rating in Israel of U.S. leaders has dropped since the Oct. 7 Hamas attack, a new survey found. Sixty-three percent of Israelis approve of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli mom killed in Jaffa terror attack while shielding 9-month-old son, Israel says
item.description: Inbar Segev-Vigder, a 33-year-old mother, died in the Jaffa terrorist attack while shielding her 9-month-old son from gunfire, Israel says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran attack on Israel 'ineffective' but a 'significant escalation': White House
item.description: The White House says that the Iranian missile attack on Israel was “defeated and ineffective" and that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know as Israel expands its operations in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes have hammered targets across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs while its military urged residents to flee more than two dozen Lebanese cities and towns, signs that Israel is ramping up its ground operations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: The new head of NATO is vowing to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and says he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance’s most powerful member.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy meet in New York as election holds high stakes for US support for Ukraine
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met face-to-face with Donald Trump on Friday with public tensions rising between the two over Ukraine's defense against Russia's invasion and in the midst of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation
